quality_note: Structural mobile-readiness audit. It confirms protective CSS exists and identifies high-risk page patterns, but real phones and browser devtools still need manual review before a definitive release.

css_results.label: Global horizontal overflow guard
css_results.why: Prevents generated tables, code, and source excerpts from widening the entire viewport.

css_results.label: Responsive breakpoints
css_results.why: Keeps layout behavior explicit across tablet, phone, and very narrow phone widths.

css_results.label: Generated grids collapse safely
css_results.why: Avoids source cards, reading routes, and visual panels overflowing on phones.

css_results.label: Tables scroll locally
css_results.why: Academic tables should scroll inside their own frame instead of breaking the page.

css_results.label: Source text expands instead of nested scrolling
css_results.why: Long primary-text pages should read naturally without forcing tiny internal scrollboxes.

css_results.label: Reader controls fit phone viewports
css_results.why: Source filters, page search, translation shortcuts, and lightbox controls must not cover the page permanently.

css_results.label: Images, diagrams, canvases, and iframes scale
css_results.why: Original figures, generated redraws, tools, and source scan embeds must stay within the viewport.

manual_review_rules: Generated source text expands naturally or wraps without clipped lines.
Tables scroll inside their own border when too wide.
Bottom reader controls do not hide navigation, citations, or next/previous links.
Source-only filtering, page search, translation shortcuts, and lightbox controls remain reachable by touch.
Diagram, scan, and tool panels fit without overlapping adjacent text.
